question: A Spring Boot 8.8 worker still has `zeebe.client.job.timeout=PT5M` in `application.yaml`. Which new property should it migrate to?

a.text: The `zeebe.client.job.*` migration table maps `zeebe.client.job.timeout` to `camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout` (env `CAMUNDA_CLIENT_WORKER_DEFAULTS_TIMEOUT`). The `defaults` stanza is where job-worker behaviour now lives.

b.text: The new namespace does not keep a top-level `job` segment; what used to be `zeebe.client.job.*` is now under `camunda.client.worker.defaults.*`.

c.text: There is no `camunda.client.worker.timeout`; the timeout is one level deeper under `worker.defaults.timeout`.

d.text: No such property exists in the 8.8 starter; `defaults` is a child of `worker`, not of `client`.

explanation: Job-worker tuning that used to live under `zeebe.client.job.*` now sits under `camunda.client.worker.defaults.*`. So a per-worker default timeout migrates from `zeebe.client.job.timeout` to `camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout`, exactly as the migration table states.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
